description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain texture and a weathered appearance. The head of the axe is forged from dark, aged metal, showing signs of wear and oxidation, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade that tapers to a sharp edge. The blade has a subtle bevel and a distinct poll at the rear, suitable for both chopping and splitting tasks. The handle is securely fitted into the eye of the axe head, likely using a traditional wedged mounting method. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, emphasizing function over form. This type of axe is commonly found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance sheds for cutting wood or general utility work.
description: A weathered hand axe with a wooden handle and a dark, worn metal blade, designed for chopping and splitting tasks.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from an angle highlighting the broad blade and the grainy, slightly curved wooden handle.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the length of the handle and the wedge-shaped profile of the blade.
description_view_2: The opposite side view shows the full extension of the handle and the thickness of the axe head.
description_view_3: The axe is viewed from the other angle, displaying the poll and the secure mounting of the blade to the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain, oval cross-section, no added grip features, traditional style.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped blade with a beveled edge and a flat poll, no beard, utilitarian design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting through the eye of the axe head.
